medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient presents after evaluation for nausea and vomiting revealed DKA. She had been complaining of myalgias, sore throat, and cough since the day prior to admission. Positive influenza test; started on Tamiflu. Placed on an insulin gtt and transferred for further evaluation.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Type 1 Diabetes Mellitus, GERD, Gastroparesis, Pyloric stenosis as infant, Hypothyroidism, Necrobiosis Lipoidica Diabeticorum
medical_history.family_history: No first degree relatives with T1DM or autoimmune disease

physical_examination.general_examination: Vital signs: T 98, BP 110/61, RR 18, SpO2 98% on RA. Ill appearing young woman sleeping in bed. Easy to wake.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological: No facial droop, spontaneously moving all four limbs; further evaluation limited by patient cooperation. Cardiovascular: RRR, no m/r/g. Lungs: CTA bilaterally. Abdomen: Normoactive bowel sounds; soft, nontender to deep palpation. Extremities: Well perfused, no edema. Skin: No obvious rashes or other lesions on limited exam.
